title: Sphalanthus prostratus
taxonomy: Sphalanthus prostratus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. This family is commonly known as the spurge family. Sphalanthus prostratus is native to tropical Africa, where it is often found in moist, subtropical forests. The classification of Sphalanthus prostratus is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the order Malpighiales. By being classified in the order Malpighiales, Sphalanthus prostratus is part of a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions.
